creator: asomers@FreeBSD.org
text:
If you create a fuse mount that has default_permissions enabled but NOT noatime, then it is possible to trigger a page fault panic when removing a CTL LUN backed by a file on this mountpoint.  The reason is that kernel consumers like CTL don't have a ucred object.  The same problem might happen with the NFS server.  I am not able to reproduce it using mdconfig to create a file-backed md device on top of fusefs.

Steps to Reproduce
==================
$ pkg install -y fusefs-ext2
$ truncate -s 1g /tmp/ext2.img
$ mkfs.ext2 /tmp/ext2.img
# Note: atime MUST be enabled
$ sudo fuse-ext2 -o default_permissions,allow_other,rw+ /tmp/ext2.img /tmp/mnt
$ sudo truncate -s 1m /tmp/mnt/file
$ sudo ctladm create -b block -o file=/tmp/mnt/file
LUN created successfully
backend:       block
device type:   0
LUN size:      1048576 bytes
blocksize      512 bytes
LUN ID:        0
Serial Number: MYSERIAL0000
Device ID:     MYDEVID0000
$ sudo ctladm port -o on -p 0
$ sudo md5 /dev/da0
$ sudo ctladm remove -b block -l 0
